title: vaccinium trichocarpum
intro: Vaccinium trichocarpum, also known as the white mountain cranberry, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to the high-altitude regions of the White Mountains in New Hampshire and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and berries have been found to have health benefits such as reducing uric acid levels and providing antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Vaccinium trichocarpum is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its herbal preparations have been used to support urinary tract health and alleviate symptoms of arthritis.
